criteria.name: controls_are_keyboard_accessible
criteria.description: All interactive controls are keyboard accessible with Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, or Space.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The Import file control is a hidden, zero-size input activated through a non-focusable label, so it is not reliably keyboard reachable. Evidence: accessibility-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: modals_manage_focus
criteria.description: Modal dialogs trap focus and return it to the originating control on close.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Opening Keyboard shortcuts left focus on the opener, and Tab moved focus to the background Tour button instead of trapping focus. Evidence: modals_manage_focus-shared.png

criteria.name: images_and_icons_have_alt_text
criteria.description: All images and icons have descriptive alt text.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Multiple rendered SVG icons have neither alt text nor accessible labels. Evidence: accessibility-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: feedback_uses_live_regions
criteria.description: Errors and notifications are conveyed both visually and via ARIA live regions.
criteria.reasoning: PASS: Copy produced a visible notification exposed through role=status and aria-live=polite. Evidence: feedback_uses_live_regions-shared.png

criteria.name: forms_have_explicit_labels
criteria.description: All form elements use explicit label elements.
criteria.reasoning: PASS: Every observed input, select, and textarea has an associated label element. Evidence: accessibility-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: headings_follow_logical_order
criteria.description: Headings follow a logical order with no skips, such as H1 to H2 to H3.
criteria.reasoning: PASS: Observed heading order is H1, H2, H2, H3, H3, H3, H4 with no skipped levels. Evidence: accessibility-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: landmark_navigation_is_present
criteria.description: Skip-to-content or landmark navigation is present.
criteria.reasoning: PASS: A Skip to content link and banner, main, section, and complementary landmarks are present. Evidence: accessibility-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: text_and_controls_have_contrast
criteria.description: All text and controls have sufficient color contrast.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Several gray text elements compute to approximately 4.34:1 contrast against the dark background, below the 4.5:1 normal-text threshold. Evidence: accessibility-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: semantic_html_roles_are_used
criteria.description: Semantic HTML roles such as nav, main, and button are used.
criteria.reasoning: PASS: The app uses semantic banner/main/section/aside landmarks, native buttons, a dialog, and labeled form controls. Evidence: accessibility-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: reduced_motion_is_respected
criteria.description: Animations respect the prefers-reduced-motion setting.
criteria.reasoning: PASS: In the reduced-motion browser, prefers-reduced-motion matched true and observed animations/transitions were disabled. Evidence: reduced_motion-shared.png

criteria.name: controls_unreachable_by_keyboard
criteria.description: Controls cannot be activated or reached via keyboard.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL condition present: The Import action is represented by a non-focusable label while its associated file input is display:none and zero-size. Evidence: accessibility-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: focus_indicators_missing_or_obscured
criteria.description: Focus indicators are missing or obscured.
criteria.reasoning: PASS condition absent: A keyboard-focused button showed a visible 2px automatic outline. Evidence: focus_indicators_missing_or_obscured-shared.png

criteria.name: custom_controls_lack_semantics
criteria.description: Custom components use non-semantic tags and lack ARIA roles or labels.
criteria.reasoning: PASS condition absent: The observed custom graph controls expose role=button and accessible names, while Import has an associated label. Evidence: accessibility-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: state_uses_color_only
criteria.description: Color-only indicators are used for error or state.
criteria.reasoning: PASS condition absent: States are conveyed with text such as noise, unresolved, confidence percentages, and diagnostic messages, not color alone. Evidence: accessibility-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: modals_fail_to_trap_focus
criteria.description: Modals do not trap keyboard focus.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL condition present: Tab from the open Keyboard shortcuts dialog moved focus to the background Tour control. Evidence: modals_manage_focus-shared.png

criteria.name: poor_contrast_makes_text_unreadable
criteria.description: Text is unreadable due to poor contrast.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL condition present: Secondary gray text was measured at approximately 4.34:1 contrast, below the normal-text AA threshold. Evidence: accessibility-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: errors_are_visual_only
criteria.description: Error feedback is visual only and is not screen-reader accessible.
criteria.reasoning: PASS condition absent: The observed notification is exposed through a polite live region; no exercised error was shown to be visual-only. Evidence: feedback_uses_live_regions-shared.png

criteria.name: media_is_missing_alt_text
criteria.description: Icon or image content is missing alt text.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL condition present: Numerous icon SVGs lack alt text, aria-label, or an equivalent accessible name. Evidence: accessibility-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: heading_order_is_skipped
criteria.description: Headings are skipped or unordered, such as jumping from H1 to H3.
criteria.reasoning: PASS condition absent: The observed heading sequence contains no skipped level. Evidence: accessibility-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: animations_ignore_reduced_motion
criteria.description: Animations ignore reduced-motion settings.
criteria.reasoning: PASS condition absent: With reduced motion forced, computed animations were none and button transitions were none. Evidence: reduced_motion-shared.png

criteria.name: accessibility_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in accessibility that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: PASS condition absent: The significant observed issues are covered by the specific keyboard, focus, contrast, modal, and alt-text criteria. Evidence: accessibility-catchall-shared.png

criteria.name: multi_facet_round_trip
criteria.description: Multi-facet round-trip: configure several independent facets (e.g. active view, a membership change, a sort order, a created item), then reload. In persistence genres, all facets survive the reload; in in-memory genres, all facets coherently reset to the seeded baseline — never a mix.
criteria.reasoning: Configured frame order and a mapped route, then reloaded; both reverted together to the seeded baseline with no route. Evidence: multi_facet_round_trip-before.png, multi_facet_round_trip-after.png

criteria.name: sort_reversal_proves_live_data
criteria.description: Sort-reversal proof: sort a collection ascending, note the order, then sort descending; the order reverses correctly relative to ascending, proving sort is derived from live data rather than two hardcoded orders.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: No ascending/descending sort control or sortable collection was available.

criteria.name: derived_view_responds_to_input
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: change an input (timeframe, filter, category) and confirm the derived view (chart, total, list) changes meaningfully rather than redrawing identically regardless of input.
criteria.reasoning: Mapping different frames changed the committed route and source excerpts from n1 to n1 → n2 → n3. Evidence: cross_view_echo_without_reload-shared.png, derived_view_responds_to_input-shared.png

criteria.name: cross_view_echo_without_reload
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: make a change in one view and confirm the same datum updates in a second, structurally different view (list ↔ detail, board ↔ stats) without reload.
criteria.reasoning: Mapping a candidate updated Graph Route and Source Excerpts immediately without reload. Evidence: cross_view_echo_without_reload-shared.png

criteria.name: count_delta_is_exact
criteria.description: Count-delta integrity: measure a count immediately before and immediately after the action that changes it; the delta matches exactly with no off-by-one or lagging counter.
criteria.reasoning: Immediately before mapping there was no route; immediately after, the committed route contained exactly n1. Evidence: count_delta_is_exact-shared.png

criteria.name: different_inputs_change_outcomes
criteria.description: Input-dependent output: perform the same workflow twice with different inputs and confirm the two outcomes differ in the ways the inputs dictate.
criteria.reasoning: Two different stack-trace inputs produced different parsed-frame structures: one noise frame versus unresolved plus frame entries. Evidence: different_inputs_change_outcomes-first.png, different_inputs_change_outcomes-second.png

criteria.name: interleaved_flows_preserve_state
criteria.description: Interleaved-flow integrity: interleave two flows (e.g. start creating item A, switch views, finish creating item B, return) and confirm neither flow corrupted the other's state.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: No two independent concurrent creation or view flows were available to interleave.

criteria.name: empty_to_repopulated_round_trip
criteria.description: Edge-state round-trip: drive a collection to empty, then repopulate; derived surfaces (totals, charts, badges) track through both transitions.
criteria.reasoning: Observed the empty no-route state, then mapped a frame and observed the route and derived source surface repopulate. Evidence: multi_facet_round_trip-after.png, count_delta_is_exact-shared.png

criteria.name: partial_persistence_is_incoherent
criteria.description: A facet of user-configured state silently reverts while others survive, producing incoherent partial persistence.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Partial persistence was not observed; the mapped route and reordered frame state reset coherently together after reload. Evidence: multi_facet_round_trip-before.png, multi_facet_round_trip-after.png

criteria.name: derived_surface_ignores_inputs
criteria.description: A derived surface renders identically regardless of its inputs.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The derived route and source excerpts changed when different frames were mapped.

criteria.name: different_inputs_produce_identical_output
criteria.description: Repeating an action with different inputs yields byte-identical output.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Different stack-trace inputs produced visibly different parsed frame counts and classifications. Evidence: different_inputs_change_outcomes-first.png, different_inputs_change_outcomes-second.png

criteria.name: cross_view_state_is_stale
criteria.description: State changes appear in one view but not another until reload.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Graph Route and Source Excerpts updated in the same live interaction without reload. Evidence: cross_view_echo_without_reload-shared.png

criteria.name: spacing_and_sizing_follow_scale
criteria.description: Spacing and sizing follow the design system's scale, with no arbitrary one-off values visible in computed styles.

criteria.name: typography_matches_spec
criteria.description: Typography matches the spec for all headings and body copy.

criteria.name: layout_matches_reference
criteria.description: Layout matches reference screenshots to within a small tolerance at all specified breakpoints; fidelity tasks achieve near pixel-parity.
criteria.reasoning: Desktop two-column composition and compact single-column stacking render coherently. Evidence: layout-desktop.png; layout-mobile.png

criteria.name: specified_state_changes_animate
criteria.description: Transitions or animations are applied to the state changes the spec calls out, such as add, delete, filter, or view switch.
criteria.reasoning: The Copy control exposes a 0.2s transition, and its hover color changes in the live browser. Evidence: hover-copy.png

criteria.name: responsive_behavior_matches_reference

criteria.name: control_styling_matches_spec

criteria.name: typography_has_clear_hierarchy

criteria.name: surface_treatments_match_spec
criteria.description: Color, depth, and border treatments are precise per the design.

criteria.name: microinteractions_match_spec

criteria.name: mobile_touch_gestures_work
criteria.description: Touch gestures such as swipe and tap work on mobile where present.
criteria.reasoning: A real tap on the mobile Show editor control completed successfully; no swipe-specific interaction is present. Evidence: mobile-responsive.png

criteria.name: small_screens_avoid_horizontal_scroll
criteria.description: Small screens have no horizontal scrolling.
criteria.reasoning: At 375px, measured document scroll width is 360px, so no horizontal overflow is present. Evidence: mobile-responsive.png

criteria.name: media_and_canvases_resize
criteria.description: Images, grids, and canvases size responsively to fit the layout.
criteria.reasoning: The code graph scales down to fit the mobile content column without overflowing. Evidence: mobile-full-responsive.png

criteria.name: fixed_controls_remain_accessible
criteria.description: Fixed controls remain accessible on all devices.
criteria.reasoning: The fixed onboarding panel remains within the mobile viewport and its controls are reachable. Evidence: mobile-responsive.png

criteria.name: layout_breaks_at_breakpoints
criteria.description: Layout breaks or overflows at common breakpoints.
criteria.reasoning: No breakpoint layout failure was observed at desktop, tablet, or mobile widths. Evidence: desktop-responsive.png, tablet-responsive.png, mobile-responsive.png

criteria.name: mobile_controls_too_small_or_misaligned
criteria.description: Controls are too small or misaligned on mobile.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: A−, A+, and the keyboard shortcut control are visibly narrower than the surrounding 44px controls. Evidence: mobile-responsive.png

criteria.name: text_too_small_on_devices
criteria.description: Text is too small to read on some devices.
criteria.reasoning: Mobile body text measures 16px and remains readable; no generally undersized device typography was observed. Evidence: mobile-responsive.png

criteria.name: content_cropped_or_unnecessary_scrollbars
criteria.description: Content is cropped or scrollbars appear unnecessarily.
criteria.reasoning: Only normal vertical scrolling for the long editor page is present; no unnecessary horizontal scrollbar or cropped main content was observed. Evidence: mobile-full-responsive.png

criteria.name: sidebar_or_menu_breaks_by_width
criteria.description: The sidebar or menu is unresponsive or missing at some widths.
criteria.reasoning: The editor/sidebar region reflows into a full-width stacked section rather than disappearing or becoming unresponsive. Evidence: tablet-responsive.png, mobile-responsive.png

criteria.name: fixed_positions_obscure_content
criteria.description: Fixed positions obscure content.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The fixed onboarding panel visibly covers editor content near the bottom of the mobile viewport. Evidence: mobile-responsive.png

criteria.name: elements_overlap_at_some_size
criteria.description: Stacking or overlap issues appear at some viewport size.
criteria.reasoning: No unintended layout-element overlap was observed; the onboarding panel is an intentional overlay. Evidence: desktop-responsive.png, tablet-responsive.png, mobile-responsive.png

criteria.name: required_mobile_touch_actions_missing
criteria.description: Touch actions are not available on mobile where the spec requires them.
criteria.reasoning: No required mobile-only touch action was specified or observed missing. Evidence: mobile-responsive.png

criteria.name: fixed_size_media_overflows
criteria.description: Images, charts, or canvases use a static size and overflow the viewport.
criteria.reasoning: The graph remains contained within the responsive content column on mobile. Evidence: mobile-full-responsive.png

criteria.name: controls_disappear_at_sizes
criteria.description: Controls disappear at specific sizes.
criteria.reasoning: Primary header and editor controls remain present at desktop, tablet, and mobile widths, with wrapping on smaller screens. Evidence: desktop-responsive.png, tablet-responsive.png, mobile-responsive.png

criteria.name: responsiveness_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in responsive layout and adaptivity that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant responsive defect beyond the explicitly identified small controls and onboarding overlay was observed. Evidence: mobile-responsive.png

criteria.name: interleave_webmcp
criteria.description: Interleave UI/WebMCP parse, candidate, path, hypothesis, annotation, and transfer operations → all linked state and checksums agree.
criteria.reasoning: UI and WebMCP operations interleaved successfully: parsed frames, mapped candidates, produced route n1→n2→n3, added annotation, saved valid hypothesis “Dispatch path,” and export/copy returned matching linked state. Evidence: interleave_webmcp-shared.png

criteria.name: create_flow_updates_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Create flow: a new item can be created via the UI and appears everywhere it should.
criteria.reasoning: A mapped route was saved as “Root cause path (valid)” and appeared in the Hypotheses list. Evidence: hypothesis-saved-shared.png

criteria.name: invalid_create_shows_inline_validation
criteria.description: Create flow: invalid submission triggers immediate inline validation.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Submitting the empty hypothesis form left the dialog open without any inline validation message. Evidence: invalid-hypothesis-shared.png

criteria.name: edit_flow_updates_related_displays
criteria.description: Edit flow: edits save and update all related displays.
criteria.reasoning: Editing the Stack Trace immediately reparsed the frames and updated the visible frame list and diagnostics. Evidence: edited-trace-shared.png

criteria.name: delete_flow_updates_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Delete flow: the item is removed from all lists/panes after deletion.
criteria.reasoning: Deleting the saved hypothesis removed it from the Hypotheses list. Evidence: hypothesis-empty-shared.png

criteria.name: view_switch_retains_state
criteria.description: View/mode switch: the view changes properly, with state retained.
criteria.reasoning: Hiding and reopening the editor retained the edited trace and its reparsed frames. Evidence: view-switch-retained-shared.png

criteria.name: last_delete_reveals_empty_state
criteria.description: Edge: after the last delete, the empty state is clear and visible.
criteria.reasoning: After deleting the only hypothesis, the clear empty-state message “No hypotheses saved yet” appeared. Evidence: hypothesis-empty-shared.png

criteria.name: filters_update_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Edge: filtering reveals/hides items everywhere as expected.
criteria.reasoning: No filtering UI/content class is offered, so no filter inconsistency was observed. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: collapsible_chrome_preserves_workflow
criteria.description: Collapsible chrome (sidebar, panel) can be hidden and reopened with workflow continuity.
criteria.reasoning: The editor could be hidden and reopened, with the edited workflow state preserved. Evidence: view-switch-retained-shared.png

criteria.name: overlays_support_expected_flows
criteria.description: Overlays behave as expected for details, edits, confirmations.
criteria.reasoning: The save dialog accepted a valid name and could be canceled; the guided-tour overlay also had a working Skip tour exit. Evidence: hypothesis-saved-shared.png

criteria.name: flow_recovers_without_reload
criteria.description: Recovery: navigation loss or error can be corrected without a reload (no dead ends).
criteria.reasoning: The blocking tour overlay was dismissed through Skip tour and the workflow continued without reload. Evidence: hypothesis-empty-shared.png

criteria.name: primary_flows_unavailable_from_ui
criteria.description: Create/edit/delete (or genre-equivalent primary flows) are not enabled from the UI.
criteria.reasoning: Create/save and delete controls were visibly available in the Hypotheses section. Evidence: hypothesis-saved-shared.png

criteria.name: new_items_missing_or_duplicated
criteria.description: New items fail to show up or duplicate.
criteria.reasoning: The newly saved hypothesis appeared exactly once in the list. Evidence: hypothesis-saved-shared.png

criteria.name: state_changes_desync_displays
criteria.description: State changes are not synced across displays.
criteria.reasoning: Mapping updated the route and source excerpt together; editing updated the parser and frame diagnostics together. Evidence: route-mapped-shared.png

criteria.name: view_switch_breaks_or_loses_selection
criteria.description: View/mode switching breaks layout or loses selection.
criteria.reasoning: Switching editor visibility did not break layout or lose the edited trace. Evidence: view-switch-retained-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_collection_breaks_ui
criteria.description: Emptying the collection produces broken or missing UI.
criteria.reasoning: Deleting the last hypothesis produced a functioning, explicit empty state. Evidence: hypothesis-empty-shared.png

criteria.name: filtered_views_disagree
criteria.description: Filtered views disagree with each other about what is visible.
criteria.reasoning: No filtering view was offered and no disagreement between filtered displays was observed. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: collapsed_chrome_cannot_reopen
criteria.description: Collapsed chrome can't be re-opened.
criteria.reasoning: The hidden editor was successfully reopened. Evidence: view-switch-retained-shared.png

criteria.name: overlay_blocks_navigation
criteria.description: Overlays block navigation with no exit option.
criteria.reasoning: The guided-tour overlay intercepted a real delete click until Skip tour was used, visibly blocking the underlying workflow. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: failed_flow_lacks_feedback
criteria.description: A failed flow provides no user feedback.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Empty hypothesis submission failed with no visible inline error or explanatory feedback. Evidence: invalid-hypothesis-shared.png

criteria.name: navigation_dead_end_requires_reload
criteria.description: Navigation dead ends or requires reload for recovery.
criteria.reasoning: The workflow recovered by dismissing the overlay and continuing without a reload. Evidence: hypothesis-empty-shared.png

criteria.name: user_flows_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in critical end-to-end user flows that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant defect beyond the covered validation and overlay-blocking issues was observed. Evidence: edited-trace-shared.png

Evidence: inspect_states-route.png, inspect_states-selected.png, inspect_states-compared.png

criteria.name: headings_use_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings and section titles, they use consistent capitalization (one convention — title case or sentence case — throughout).
criteria.reasoning: Headings consistently use title case throughout the rendered app. Evidence: copy-desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they are specific verbs ('Add expense', 'Start session'), not generic ('Submit', 'OK') when a specific label is possible.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The guided tour uses the generic action label “Next” where a more specific label could be used. Evidence: copy-desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: errors_name_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders error messages, they name the problem and the fix ('Amount must be a positive number'), not just a rejection ('Invalid').
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The unresolved/error state identifies “asdf” as unresolved but provides no corrective guidance or fix. Evidence: copy-invalid-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders empty states, the copy explains what belongs there and how to add it.
criteria.reasoning: Empty states explain both what is missing and how to proceed, such as mapping candidates or adding annotations. Evidence: copy-desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: body_copy_is_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders body or marketing copy, rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors.
criteria.reasoning: Copy is generally clear and grammatical, with a minor visible spacing defect in “f8 (frame)wrapper.” Evidence: copy-desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: terminology_is_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders labels for the same concept in multiple places, terminology is consistent (not 'task' here and 'todo' there).
criteria.reasoning: Core concepts use consistent terms such as frames, candidates, routes, nodes, and annotations. Evidence: copy-desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: numbers_dates_and_units_are_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders numbers, dates, and units, formatting is consistent (same date format, same decimal precision, units labeled).
criteria.reasoning: Confidence percentages, line numbers, node/edge counts, and weights are formatted consistently and labeled appropriately. Evidence: copy-desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: success_messages_are_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmation and success messages, they state what happened ('Entry saved'), not vague affirmations.
criteria.reasoning: The copy confirmation states exactly what happened: “Session JSON copied to clipboard.” Evidence: copy-copy-result-shared.png

criteria.name: placeholder_copy_is_visible
criteria.description: Lorem ipsum or placeholder text ('TODO', 'Lorem', 'asdf', or template variable names such as '{{title}}') is visible anywhere in the shipped UI.
criteria.reasoning: No Lorem ipsum, TODO, template variables, or other shipped placeholder text was observed. Evidence: copy-desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: prominent_copy_has_errors
criteria.description: Spelling or grammatical errors appear in prominent copy such as headings, hero text, or primary buttons.
criteria.reasoning: No spelling or grammatical errors were observed in prominent headings, hero copy, or primary actions. Evidence: copy-desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: concept_names_are_inconsistent
criteria.description: The same concept is named inconsistently across surfaces.
criteria.reasoning: No inconsistent naming of the same concept across visible surfaces was observed. Evidence: copy-desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: copy_is_truncated_or_overflowing
criteria.description: Truncated or overflowing text renders copy unreadable.
criteria.reasoning: Rendered copy remains readable; no visible truncation or overflow was observed. Evidence: copy-desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: writing_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in rendered copy quality that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No significant additional copy-quality defect beyond the minor spacing issue already noted was observed. Evidence: copy-desktop-shared.png
